# Iteration 031: ADOPTION ROADMAP DRAFT

## Focus
ADOPTION ROADMAP DRAFT: turn the prior 30 iterations into a Q1/Q2/Q3 adoption sequence for Spec Kit Memory, with explicit dependencies between the adopted Mex patterns.

## Findings
### Finding 1: Q1 foundation should be a separate markdown integrity lane
- **Source**: [README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L72), [claims.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/claims.ts#L36), [path.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/path.ts#L8), [edges.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/edges.ts#L5), [index-sync.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/index-sync.ts#L6), [research.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md#L268592)
- **What it does**: Mex extracts typed markdown claims, then runs cheap static checks for missing paths, dead edges, and pattern-index drift before any repair loop.
- **Why it matters**: This is the cleanest gap versus current Spec Kit Memory, and prior synthesis already ranked it as the top adopt-now slice.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now, Q1 milestone
- **Dependencies**: none; this is the base layer every later pattern consumes
- **Impact**: high
- **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 2: Q1 should also standardize a tiny guided maintenance surface
- **Source**: [README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L92), [cli.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/cli.ts#L8), [SYNC.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/SYNC.md#L1), [research.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md#L268605)
- **What it does**: Mex keeps the operator surface small: `check`, `sync`, `init`, `watch`, `commands`, with quiet/json/manual fallback paths.
- **Why it matters**: Prior iterations repeatedly converged on the DX problem being fragmentation, not missing primitives.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now, Q1 milestone
- **Dependencies**: ideally ships on top of Finding 1 so the public surface points to real integrity outputs
- **Impact**: high
- **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 3: Q2 should introduce a provider-neutral `spec-kit doctor`
- **Source**: [README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L96), [sync/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts#L29), [brief-builder.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts#L7), [research.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md#L268616)
- **What it does**: Mex’s core loop is `detect -> build repair brief -> fix -> recheck -> repeat`, with file-scoped evidence and closure checks each cycle.
- **Why it matters**: The prior 30 iterations already concluded this is the strongest reusable DX pattern, but it only becomes safe once integrity diagnostics and public verbs are stable.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE, Q2 milestone
- **Dependencies**: requires Finding 1 diagnostic schemas and Finding 2 operator-facing surface
- **Impact**: high
- **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 4: Q2 should prototype freshness as advisory trust metadata
- **Source**: [staleness.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts#L1), [git.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/git.ts#L12), [reporter.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/reporter.ts#L16), [research.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md#L268627)
- **What it does**: Mex assigns stale status from days-since-change and commits-since-change using fixed thresholds, then renders that in the health output.
- **Why it matters**: Prior research accepted the dual-signal idea but rejected turning it into truth; the right adoption path is metadata, hints, or provenance weighting.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later, Q2 milestone
- **Dependencies**: depends on Finding 1 so staleness augments verified integrity; integrates best through Finding 3 rather than as a separate gate
- **Impact**: medium
- **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 5: Q3 should treat scaffold growth as an optional companion layer
- **Source**: [AGENTS.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/AGENTS.md#L29), [ROUTER.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/ROUTER.md#L56), [pattern/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/pattern/index.ts#L6), [research.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md#L268636)
- **What it does**: Mex grows patterns and current-state docs after each task, keeping markdown playbooks alive through a GROW loop.
- **Why it matters**: The prior iterations consistently treated this as useful but secondary: good for operational playbooks, bad as the default durable-memory substrate.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later, Q3 milestone
- **Dependencies**: depends on Finding 1 to prevent doc drift, and on Finding 3 to provide a maintenance loop that keeps the companion layer honest
- **Impact**: medium
- **Source strength**: primary

- [ROUTER.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/ROUTER.md#L56)

## Assessment
- New information ratio: 0.16
- Questions addressed: quarter sequencing, dependency ordering, where `spec-kit doctor` fits, whether freshness precedes or follows integrity, when scaffold growth becomes safe
- Questions answered: Q1 = integrity lane plus small maintenance surface; Q2 = `spec-kit doctor` plus advisory freshness metadata; Q3 = optional scaffold-growth companion after integrity and doctor exist
- Novelty justification: the mechanisms were already known by iteration 030, but this pass adds the missing dependency graph and milestone timing
- Dependency chain: `integrity lane -> guided maintenance surface -> spec-kit doctor -> advisory freshness -> optional scaffold-growth companion`

## Ruled Out
- markdown-first memory replacement, because prior synthesis concluded it would regress hybrid retrieval and causal/context tooling
- single blended drift score as the main health surface, because subsystem evidence matters more than one aggregate number
- Q1 adoption of command/dependency/script-coverage audits, because iteration 030 already recommended waiting for a provider-neutral inventory model

## Reflection
- What worked: using the final synthesis in [research.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md#L268592) as the roadmap anchor, then validating each quarter against primary Mex source files
- What did not work: the phase artifacts are noisy and partially duplicated, CocoIndex calls canceled during this pass, and the read-only sandbox prevented persisting this iteration into the phase folder
- What I would do differently: turn the Q1 dependency contract into a small acceptance matrix first, so later iterations can validate execution readiness instead of re-deriving sequencing

## Recommended Next Focus
Convert Q1 into implementation-ready slices: define the markdown-integrity issue schema, the operator-facing command/hint surface, and the explicit handoff contract that `spec-kit doctor` will consume in Q2.

# Iteration 031: ADOPTION ROADMAP DRAFT

## Focus
ADOPTION ROADMAP DRAFT: turn the prior 30 iterations into a Q1/Q2/Q3 adoption sequence for Spec Kit Memory, with explicit dependencies between the adopted Mex patterns.

## Findings
### Finding 1: Q1 foundation should be a separate markdown integrity lane
- **Source**: [README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L72), [claims.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/claims.ts#L36), [path.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/path.ts#L8), [edges.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/edges.ts#L5), [index-sync.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/index-sync.ts#L6), [research.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md#L268592)
- **What it does**: Mex extracts typed markdown claims, then runs cheap static checks for missing paths, dead edges, and pattern-index drift before any repair loop.
- **Why it matters**: This is the cleanest gap versus current Spec Kit Memory, and prior synthesis already ranked it as the top adopt-now slice.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now, Q1 milestone
- **Dependencies**: none; this is the base layer every later pattern consumes
- **Impact**: high
- **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 2: Q1 should also standardize a tiny guided maintenance surface
- **Source**: [README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L92), [cli.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/cli.ts#L8), [SYNC.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/SYNC.md#L1), [research.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md#L268605)
- **What it does**: Mex keeps the operator surface small: `check`, `sync`, `init`, `watch`, `commands`, with quiet/json/manual fallback paths.
- **Why it matters**: Prior iterations repeatedly converged on the DX problem being fragmentation, not missing primitives.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now, Q1 milestone
- **Dependencies**: ideally ships on top of Finding 1 so the public surface points to real integrity outputs
- **Impact**: high
- **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 3: Q2 should introduce a provider-neutral `spec-kit doctor`
- **Source**: [README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L96), [sync/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/index.ts#L29), [brief-builder.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/sync/brief-builder.ts#L7), [research.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md#L268616)
- **What it does**: Mex’s core loop is `detect -> build repair brief -> fix -> recheck -> repeat`, with file-scoped evidence and closure checks each cycle.
- **Why it matters**: The prior 30 iterations already concluded this is the strongest reusable DX pattern, but it only becomes safe once integrity diagnostics and public verbs are stable.
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE, Q2 milestone
- **Dependencies**: requires Finding 1 diagnostic schemas and Finding 2 operator-facing surface
- **Impact**: high
- **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 4: Q2 should prototype freshness as advisory trust metadata
- **Source**: [staleness.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/checkers/staleness.ts#L1), [git.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/git.ts#L12), [reporter.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/reporter.ts#L16), [research.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md#L268627)
- **What it does**: Mex assigns stale status from days-since-change and commits-since-change using fixed thresholds, then renders that in the health output.
- **Why it matters**: Prior research accepted the dual-signal idea but rejected turning it into truth; the right adoption path is metadata, hints, or provenance weighting.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later, Q2 milestone
- **Dependencies**: depends on Finding 1 so staleness augments verified integrity; integrates best through Finding 3 rather than as a separate gate
- **Impact**: medium
- **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 5: Q3 should treat scaffold growth as an optional companion layer
- **Source**: [AGENTS.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/AGENTS.md#L29), [ROUTER.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/ROUTER.md#L56), [pattern/index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/pattern/index.ts#L6), [research.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md#L268636)
- **What it does**: Mex grows patterns and current-state docs after each task, keeping markdown playbooks alive through a GROW loop.
- **Why it matters**: The prior iterations consistently treated this as useful but secondary: good for operational playbooks, bad as the default durable-memory substrate.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later, Q3 milestone
- **Dependencies**: depends on Finding 1 to prevent doc drift, and on Finding 3 to provide a maintenance loop that keeps the companion layer honest
- **Impact**: medium
- **Source strength**: primary
